metric_value: Price/performance advantage over IBM 3090 mainframes; targets high-end commercial OLTP
notes: Aberdeen recommends Encore target people-to-computer OLTP over computer-to-computer OLTP

metric_name: Total high-end OLTP market size (>25 TPS, 1989)
metric_value: $1.4 billion
notes: Aberdeen estimate for systems with application requirements exceeding 25 TPS

metric_name: Total high-end OLTP market size (>100 TPS, 1990)
metric_value: $630 million
notes: Aberdeen estimate; basis for top-10 priority market targets

metric_value: 2,000 TPS peak; 200 TPS per IBM 3090-200; growing at 15% per annum
notes: American's SABRE—largest cited OLTP application

metric_name: Credit authorization peak TPS (VISA/MasterCard positive auth)
metric_value: 200 TPS
notes: American Express negative auth cited at 150 TPS at Christmas peak on $100K midrange

metric_value: #3 of 10 priority markets; estimated $200M high-end OLTP market
notes: UNIX + price/performance well-suited for administrative and national security applications

metric_value: ~40 TPS; $8M five-year lifecycle cost
notes: Aberdeen's baseline for comparing Encore Series 90 price/performance

metric_name: Commercial Series 90 market viability as IBM mainframe alternative
metric_value: Aberdeen believes commercial Series 90 can address wider database/data-communications commercial markets with outstanding price/performance in multiples of IBM mainframes
notes: Prediction: Encore would successfully compete against IBM 3090 mainframes

metric_value: Encore failed commercially; Series 90 did not displace IBM mainframes; company liquidated January 1999
notes: Encore sold storage business to Sun for $185M (1997); real-time business sold to Gores Technology Group for $3M (1998)

metric_value: $40M estimated; call switching 400 TPS growing to 600 TPS in 5 years
notes: Service order processing and maintenance processing as primary targets

metric_value: $50M estimated
notes: Order entry and inventory control as primary targets

metric_name: Banking retail OLTP priority #8 market size
metric_value: $65M estimated; 125 TPS mainframe norm in Europe
notes: European 1992 economic changes seen as opportunity for non-national-vendor systems

metric_name: Social Security Administration OLTP volume estimate
metric_value: 100 TPS aggregate over 10,000 terminals
notes: Antiquated system cited as requiring replacement with modern OLTP-based system

metric_name: Massively parallel computing for information retrieval
metric_value: Thinking Machines installed at Dow Jones for full-text retrieval; early commercial deployment
notes: Thinking Machines later dissolved; assets acquired by Sun Microsystems 1994

metric_value: Sears/IBM Prodigy expects 100 TPS within three years
notes: Prodigy service grew but never reached these volumes; service discontinued 2001

metric_name: Object-oriented database adoption by insurance industry
metric_value: Aberdeen believes insurance industry will increasingly embrace digital imaging and object-oriented databases
notes: OODB adoption was limited; insurance industry largely adopted RDBMS instead

metric_value: RDBMS performance has been poor until recently; cannot be price/performance justified for decision support with real-time query requirements
notes: This limitation dissolved rapidly with Oracle, Sybase, Ingres improvements 1990-1994

metric_value: UNIX is highly desirable for military COTS OLTP; intelligence analysis, logistics, and decision systems
notes: Prediction proved accurate—UNIX dominated mission-critical government computing in 1990s

metric_value: DebitCredit evolving as de facto standard for measuring OLTP performance; TPS at 95th percentile <1 second response
notes: DebitCredit was formalized as TPC-A by Transaction Processing Council in 1989

metric_value: Six factors: demonstrable high-end processing requirements; market barriers to entry; entrenched competition; financial attractiveness; time to market for new applications; market innovation or risk aversion tendencies
notes: Aberdeen's proprietary framework for ranking vertical market opportunities
